# Welcome {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| include: false
# This chunk activates knitr for inline code
```

This online resource shares materials from the "Integrating Generative AI into R Workflows: From APIs to Shiny Apps" workshop, first presented at first [AIMECON](https://ncme.org/events/aime-conference/) meeting in October, 2025. The second iteration of this workshop is on Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at the [NCME Annual Meeting in Los Angeles.](https://ncme.org/events/2026-annual-meeting/)

The workshop assumes some working knowledge of the R programming language, and assumes little generative AI knowledge. If you need more information on using R, I highly recommend Hadley Wickham's (free) [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/).

This website licensed under the [CC BY-NC-ND 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/deed.en) License. 

Changes to the book last made on: 

**`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')`** (GMT)

***

<div style="font-size: 1.75em; font-weight: bold; margin-top: 1em; color: black;">
Learning Objectives
</div>

[By the end of this workshop, participants will be able to:]{.underline}

-	Explain key LLM architectural features that inform effective integration practices
-	Implement both conversational and transactional API interactions with LLMs from R
-	Design and deploy simple chained prompt workflows for complex tasks
-	Build interactive Shiny applications that leverage LLM capabilities for end-user functionality

<a href="downloads/workshop-notebooks.zip" download 
   style="display: inline-block; padding: 10px 20px; background-color: #4A90E2; 
          color: black; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 5px;">
  📥 Download All Activity Notebooks
</a>

***

<div style="font-size: 1.75em; font-weight: bold; margin-top: 1em; color: black;">
⚠️ Privacy Reminder
</div>

Please don’t enter any personal, confidential, or sensitive information into generative AI tools. This includes private details about yourself or others, as well as proprietary data. Assume that anything you input could be used to train future models or be visible to others. It is important for you to consult both your IT departments and legal counsel before using a generative AI model in any operational setting.

***

<div style="font-size: 1.75em; font-weight: bold; margin-top: 1em; color: black;">
⚖️ Legal Disclaimer
</div>
The information provided in this resource is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. You should not act upon any information presented without first seeking qualified legal counsel regarding your specific situation. The author disclaims any liability for actions taken based on the content provided here.
